notes: PARTIAL / SIM-vs-METAL DIVERGENCE (the headline field.* finding). On real ESP-NOW the §4.3.4 reply-confirmed distributed trail converges to directed_via ONLY on the hop ADJACENT to the destination (R2->D, 20/20 clean) — the upstream hops (A->R1, R1->R2) stay FLOODING even after 20 reply round-trips with clean isolated traffic (demo Events gated off) and exactly-once delivery. The conjecture (sim 8/8) asserts 'after reply round-trips EVERY on-path node forwards directed toward D via its own next-hop' = end-to-end. Metal refutes the end-to-end claim while CONFIRMING: adjacent-hop convergence, exactly_once, and the black-hole/alt-path no-steal property. STRUCTURAL HYPOTHESIS: R2<->D is an unambiguous reverse link (D's only neighbour is R2) so the reply strong-reinforces R2->D directly; upstream the reply FLOODS back (A/R1 forward paths aren't converged), so the strong-reinforcement toward D arrives via varying senders / competes with flood-churn / decays before crossing the directed threshold. SPEC-FIRST: routed to specs (conjecture owner) + core (r2_route::trail policy owner) BEFORE any firmware change — is this (a) a real §4.3.4 sim-vs-metal divergence needing a spec refinement (e.g. reply must pin the reverse next-hop, or a stronger upstream reinforce), (b) a refutation of the end-to-end claim, or (c) a subtle issue in hive's reply-propagation/src_hive/forwarded-set wiring that core should review. Reused the REAL RouteEngine + r2_route::trail (drift-gate-faithful to sim) so the divergence is in the policy-on-real-RF or hive's call-sites, not a reimpl. Raw serial attached for re-decision.
